channel.title: NYT > U.S. News
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: 5 Takeaways From the California Gubernatorial Debate
item.description: Seven candidates sparred in the first nationally televised debate in the 2026 California governor’s race. Many tried to launch attacks in a last-ditch effort to gain ground on their opponents.
item.dc:creator: Laurel Rosenhall and Jill Cowan
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Seven candidates engaged in a spirited debate hosted by CNN at East Los Angeles College on Tuesday.

item.title: Illinois State Police to Investigate Fatal ICE Shooting
item.description: Officials said they were examining the shooting of Silverio Villegas-Gonzalez last summer during a Chicago-area crackdown on illegal immigration.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
item.media:description: A memorial for Silverio Villegas-Gonzalez, who was shot by an ICE agent in Franklin Park, Ill., last year.

item.title: Democrats Keep Michigan Senate Majority With Special Election Win
item.description: Gov. Gretchen Whitmer, a Democrat, allowed the swing district seat to sit vacant for more than a year as her party clung to a slim majority.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith and Sam Easter

item.title: Suspect in Colorado Firebombing Attack Will Plead Guilty, Court Records Say
item.description: Mohamed Sabry Soliman, who was charged in an attack that injured more than a dozen people and killed one, will be sentenced to life in prison, according to court documents.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra
item.media:credit: Lisa Turnquist, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Mohamed Sabry Soliman, at right, during the attack last year.

item.title: Takeaways From Indiana Primary Elections 2026: Trump Gets Payback
item.description: The president helped unseat most of the state lawmakers he targeted after they rebuffed his call to draw new House maps to help Republicans.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith and Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times
item.media:description: The Indiana Statehouse in Indianapolis.

item.title: U.S. Military Strikes Boat in Eastern Pacific, Killing 3
item.description: The attack was the second in two days, and the latest in an ongoing campaign against people the United States says are engaged in drug smuggling at sea.
item.dc:creator: Adam Sella
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: U.S. Southern Command, led by Gen. Francis L. Donovan of the Marine Corps, announced the attack on Tuesday evening.

item.title: Ex-ICE Deputy Falls Short in Republican House Primary in Ohio
item.description: Madison Sheahan, whose candidacy promised to test how general-election voters viewed President Trump’s immigration agenda, lost to a rival who billed himself as a “MAGA Republican.”
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Tom Williams/CQ Roll Call, via Associated Press; Leah Millis/Reuters
item.media:description: Madison D. Sheahan, right, stepped down from U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement to run for the House in Ohio, but she was defeated in the Republican primary race by Derek Merrin, left, a former state legislator.

item.title: Trump and Rubio Insist Iran War Is Over, Even as Missiles Fly During Cease-Fire
item.description: The White House is turning to rhetorical leaps as President Trump tries to put the biggest political crisis of his presidency behind him.
item.dc:creator: David E. Sanger
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Despite Secretary of State Marco Rubio’s declaration that the objectives of the war have been accomplished, they clearly have not.

item.title: A Solitary Protester on Top of the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge in D.C.
item.description: For five days, a 45-year-old former jeweler has camped on top of the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge, drawing a mix of support, disapproval and concern.
item.dc:creator: Chris Hippensteel and JoAnna Daemmrich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Ken Cedeno/Reuters
item.media:description: Guido Reichstadter atop the Frederick Douglass Memorial Bridge in Washington, D.C. on Saturday. His protest there has gone on longer than he had planned.

item.title: Trump Posts Meme of Top Democrats in Sombreros
item.description: The fake image of Representative Hakeem Jeffries of New York and Senator Chuck Schumer of New York was similar to a post from September that was widely denounced as racist.
item.dc:creator: Erica L. Green
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Hakeem Jeffries, the House minority leader, and Chuck Schumer, the Senate minority leader, outside the White House after meeting with President Trump in September.

item.title: In Indiana Primary Elections, Most Trump-Backed Challengers Beat Incumbents
item.description: President Trump endorsed primary opponents against seven Republican lawmakers who opposed his redistricting push. At least five of the Trump-backed candidates have won their races.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith
item.media:credit: Cara Penquite/Associated Press
item.media:description: Voters at the polls in West Lafayette, Ind., on Tuesday.

item.title: Vance Campaigns in Iowa as G.O.P. Fears Rise Ahead of Midterms
item.description: The vice president acknowledged economic headwinds, including rising energy and fertilizer costs. “We got a little — a little blip in the Middle East,” he said, referring to the war in Iran.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher
item.media:credit: Charlie Neibergall/Associated Press
item.media:description: Vice President JD Vance speaking during a visit to Ex-Guard Industries on Tuesday in Des Moines.

item.title: Ohio Governor’s Race Set Between Vivek Ramaswamy and Amy Acton
item.description: In a close race, Vivek Ramaswamy, the Republican nominee, and Dr. Amy Acton, the Democrat, bring distinctive potential liabilities: his wealth and her time leading Ohio’s pandemic response.
item.dc:creator: Campbell Robertson

item.title: U.S. Allows Venezuela to Begin Debt Restructuring Process
item.description: Venezuela must repay $60 billion in defaulted bonds as it looks to stabilize its economy.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.media:credit: Todd Heisler/The New York Times
item.media:description: Streets in Venezuela’s capital, Caracas, last week. The Trump administration has said that it has started sending millions of dollars of oil revenue to Venezuela’s government.

item.title: Man Accused of Attacking White House Correspondents Dinner Indicted on Four Charges
item.description: A grand jury approved a fourth count against Cole Tomas Allen, who prosecutors say wounded a federal officer while attempting to kill President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Zach Montague
item.media:credit: Salwan Georges for The New York Times
item.media:description: Law enforcement officers responded to shots fired during the White House Correspondents’ Association dinner.

item.title: F.A.A. Employee Charged With Threatening to Kill Trump
item.description: The employee, Dean DelleChiaie of Nashua, N.H., was accused of emailing the White House about his plans to kill the president, after using his work computer to conduct related searches.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: An employee of the Federal Aviation Administration made his plans to harm President Trump explicit in April, in an email from his personal account sent to the White House’s public email address.

item.title: What Happened When Pope Leo XIV Had to Call Customer Service
item.description: Spoiler alert: There was no miracle.
item.dc:creator: Julie Bosman
item.media:credit: Alessandra Tarantino/Associated Press
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV has a palace, the Swiss Guard and the popemobile. But he also has hassles, just like everyone else.

item.title: Suspect in National Mall Shooting Expressed Hostility Toward White House, Authorities Say
item.description: Michael Marx, a 45-year-old Texan, is accused of shooting at Secret Service agents by the Washington Monument on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Minho Kim, Glenn Thrush, Jacey Fortin and Jin Yu Young
item.media:credit: Andrew Leyden/Getty Images
item.media:description: A Secret Service agent standing guard at a security perimeter on the National Mall after the shooting on Monday.

item.title: To Rename Its Airport After Trump, Palm Beach Had to License His Name
item.description: The Trumps won’t profit from airport-branded merchandise sold on site. But the agreement doesn’t prevent them from profiting off such items sold elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Patricia Mazzei
item.media:credit: Haiyun Jiang for The New York Times
item.media:description: Originally opened in 1936 as Morrison Field, the Florida airport was renamed Palm Beach International Airport in 1948.

item.title: F.A.A. Proposes Rule for Drone-Free Zones
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration proposed a system for designating certain “critical infrastructure” sites off limits to unmanned aircraft.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.media:credit: Aj Mast/Associated Press
item.media:description: A drone flying over Memorial Stadium in Bloomington, Ind., before a college football game.

item.title: Ex-FedEx Driver Who Killed 7-Year-Old Athena Strand Is Sentenced to Death
item.description: Tanner Horner kidnapped and strangled the girl, Athena Strand, after delivering a package to her home in Texas. The package was her Christmas gift, a set of Barbies.
item.dc:creator: Rylee Kirk
item.media:credit: Shafkat Anowar/The Dallas Morning News, via Associated Press
item.media:description: A makeshift memorial for Athena Strand in Paradise, Texas, in 2022. She was kidnapped from outside her father’s home.

item.title: Judge Refers Justice Dept. Lawyer for Possible Discipline, Calling Out ‘Lack of Candor’
item.description: Officials withheld a detainee’s overseas arrest warrant from a federal judge. When she ordered his release, they used the same information to attack her publicly.
item.dc:creator: Mattathias Schwartz
item.media:credit: USA Today Network, via Reuters Connect
item.media:description: Judge Melissa R. DuBose, left, after being sworn in as a U.S. district judge in Cranston, R.I., last year.

item.title: F.D.A. Blocked Publication of Research Finding Covid and Shingles Vaccines Were Safe
item.description: The agency’s scientists and data contractors reviewed millions of patient records for studies that were pulled back before release.
item.dc:creator: Christina Jewett
item.category: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
item.media:credit: Kim Raff for The New York Times
item.media:description: A Covid-19 vaccine distribution site in Salt Lake City in 2022.

item.title: G.O.P. Proposes $1 Billion in Immigration Bill for Trump’s Ballroom Project
item.description: The money would go toward security improvements as part of an East Wing construction project, including a new ballroom that President Trump has said would be built with private dollars.
item.dc:creator: Carl Hulse
item.media:credit: Doug Mills/The New York Times
item.media:description: Construction at the White House last month.

item.title: The Cheap Guided Rockets U.S. Forces Use Against Iranian Drones
item.description: Called the Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System, it adds laser guidance to a weapon first used in the Korean War.
item.dc:creator: John Ismay
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Justin Tallis/Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: An Advanced Precision Kill Weapon System on display at an air show in London in 2024.

item.title: Secret Service Agent John Spillman Charged With Indecent Exposure at Miami Hotel
item.description: The officer, who was in Miami for an event President Trump was attending over the weekend, was seen masturbating in the hallway, the police said.
item.dc:creator: Pooja Salhotra and Sonia A. Rao

item.title: Trump Administration Investigating Smith College Over Transgender Admissions
item.description: The Education Department’s civil rights arm said admitting “biological males” to the women’s college may violate anti-discrimination laws.
item.dc:creator: Michael C. Bender
item.media:credit: Christopher Capozziello for The New York Times
item.media:description: Smith College, one of the nation’s largest single-sex schools with about 2,500 students, has been admitting transgender students since 2015, along with several other top women’s colleges.

item.title: A Climate Activist Wants to Run California. Coal Helped Fuel His Wealth.
item.description: Questions linger about the finances of Tom Steyer, who has emerged as a leading contender in the California governor’s race. He says he has asked that his money be separated from fossil fuel investments.
item.dc:creator: Danny Hakim, Julie Tate and Laurel Rosenhall
item.media:credit: Fred Greaves/Reuters
item.media:description: Tom Steyer has become a leading contender in the California governor’s race since the sudden withdrawal of Eric Swalwell last month.

item.title: U.S.C. Will Infuse A.I. Across University with $200 Million Donation
item.description: The University of Southern California wants to integrate artificial intelligence into fields like health care and the arts. Other universities are also using donations to focus more on A.I.
item.dc:creator: Alan Blinder
item.media:credit: Kendrick Brinson for The New York Times
item.media:description: The University of Southern California campus in Los Angeles.

item.title: What to Know About the California Primary Election
item.description: Voters are starting to make decisions after receiving their primary ballots this week. The election includes a turbulent governor’s race and a competitive mayoral contest in Los Angeles.
item.dc:creator: Jill Cowan
item.media:credit: Gabriela Bhaskar/The New York Times
item.media:description: Voters are starting to tune in to the June 2 primary in California.

item.title: Paxton’s Fund-Raising Struggles in Texas Underscore Deep Rift in G.O.P.
item.description: The Texas attorney general is heading into the runoff against Senator John Cornyn without the support of some of his longtime backers.
item.dc:creator: Lauren McGaughy
item.media:credit: Desiree Rios for The New York Times
item.media:description: On the campaign trail for U.S. Senate, the Texas attorney general, Ken Paxton, has presented his fund-raising gap as a strength.

item.title: President Trump Seeks Retribution in Republican Primaries
item.description: President Trump is endorsing candidates in a series of primary elections this month, trying to defeat incumbents in his own party who have crossed him. Our national political correspondent Shane Goldmacher explains.
item.dc:creator: Shane Goldmacher, Jon Miller, Paul Abowd and Luke Piotrowski

item.title: In Ohio, Indiana and Michigan’s Primary Elections, Trump’s Influence Faces a Test
item.description: Indiana voters were deciding whether to support Republican state lawmakers who rejected the president’s push to redraw the state’s political map last year. Mr. Trump backed challengers.
item.dc:creator: Mitch Smith and Kellen Browning
item.media:credit: Jon Cherry for The New York Times
item.media:description: Indiana state legislators met in Indianapolis last year before a vote on redrawing the state’s congressional map.

item.title: Ohio’s 2026 Senate Primary Election Kicks Off With Attacks About Epstein
item.description: Ohio’s unusual contest — with a little-known incumbent and a well-known challenger — shows how Democrats are hoping to capitalize on G.O.P. voters’ anger at the Jeffrey Epstein scandal.
item.dc:creator: Reid J. Epstein
item.media:credit: Maddie McGarvey for The New York Times; Kenny Holston/The New York Times
item.media:description: Former Senator Sherrod Brown of Ohio, left, is running again for the chamber against Senator Jon Husted, a Republican who was appointed to his seat by the governor to replace Vice President JD Vance.
